title: Earnings: As of March 31, 2026
content:
Northern Valley reported net income of $45K as of March 31, 2026. This figure reflects the continued strength of our core lending and investment activities despite a challenging quarter for interest margins. Interest income totaled $234K while interest expense amounted to $43K, generating spread income that softened to levels management anticipated given current market conditions. The earnings level underscores the durability of our primary earnings engine as we navigate this period.

Non-interest income totaled $33K and continues to provide a modest but reliable complement to core earnings. The majority of this income is generated from other income (includes unrealized gains/losses) at $0, with additional contributions from fee income at $174K. These revenue streams support diversification even in the face of headwinds.

Non-interest expense totaled $182K reflecting continued discipline in managing operating costs and aligned with our investment in member service quality. The largest component is credit loss expense at $3K, followed by loan servicing expense at $63K and employee compensation and benefits at $70K. Overall expense stability remains consistent as we balance operational needs against the scale of services provided.

Overall Position:
Northern Valley continues to generate managed earnings supported by interest income from our lending portfolio. Non-interest income plays a supportive role while expenses remain under control through disciplined cost management. Sustaining performance will depend on maintaining these core drivers and monitoring market conditions closely. We are confident in the institution's financial trajectory as we move forward.
